SHEVA BERACHOS שֶׁבַע בְּרָכוֹת ZIMUN · 290 בָּרוּךְ אַתָּה יְהוָה SEVEN BLESSINGS אָמֵן LITURGICAL SERIES · NO.02 אֱלֹהֵינוּ מֶלֶךְ הָעוֹלָם FOLLOWORBOUNCE.COM חָתָן וְכַלָּה BRIDE + GROOM שָׂשׂוֹן וְשִׂמְחָה SHEVA BERACHOS שֶׁבַע בְּרָכוֹת ZIMUN · 290 בָּרוּךְ אַתָּה יְהוָה SEVEN BLESSINGS אָמֵן LITURGICAL SERIES · NO.02 אֱלֹהֵינוּ מֶלֶךְ הָעוֹלָם FOLLOWORBOUNCE.COM חָתָן וְכַלָּה BRIDE + GROOM שָׂשׂוֹן וְשִׂמְחָה

P.290
ז
NO.02
291 / SHEVA BERACHOS שבע ברכות — Seven Blessings זִמּוּן / 290
בָּרוּךְ
Zimun — Leader recites

שֶׁבַע
בְּרָכוֹת.

‹ bring salvation ‹ VAHO ‹ ANI «« let us bless ‹ Gentlemen ‹ Blessed ‹ are You ‹ Hashem ‹ our God ‹ King ‹ of the universe ‹ Who has created everything ‹ for His glory ‹ Who fashioned the Man ‹ in His image ‹ in His semblance ‹ bring salvation ‹ VAHO ‹ ANI «« let us bless ‹ Gentlemen ‹ Blessed ‹ are You ‹ Hashem ‹ our God ‹ King ‹ of the universe ‹ Who has created everything ‹ for His glory ‹ Who fashioned the Man ‹ in His image ‹ in His semblance

Baruch Atah Adonai Eloheinu Melech Ha'olam —
Blessed are You, Lord our God, King of the universe.

Blessing 1 of 7

שֶׁהַכֹּל בָּרָא לִכְבוֹדוֹ

Who has created everything for His glory

Blessing 2 of 7

יוֹצֵר הָאָדָם

Who fashioned the Man

Blessing 4 of 7

שׂוֹשׂ תָּשִׂישׂ

May she rejoice intensely — the barren one

Blessing 7 of 7

בּוֹרֵא פְּרִי הַגָּפֶן

Who creates the fruit of the vine

AFTER THE RING
KESUBAH READ
SECOND CUP POURED
7 BLESSINGS RECITED
GROOM + BRIDE DRINK
‹ delight ‹ pleasure ‹ glad song ‹ rejoicing ‹ and gladness ‹ groom and bride ‹ joy ‹ love ‹ brotherhood ‹ peace ‹ companionship ‹ eternity «« let there be heard ‹ in the cities of Judah ‹ in the streets of Jerusalem ‹ sound of joy ‹ sound of gladness ‹ voice of groom ‹ voice of bride ‹ delight ‹ pleasure ‹ glad song ‹ rejoicing ‹ and gladness ‹ groom and bride ‹ joy ‹ love ‹ brotherhood ‹ peace ‹ companionship ‹ eternity «« let there be heard ‹ in the cities of Judah ‹ in the streets of Jerusalem ‹ sound of joy ‹ sound of gladness ‹ voice of groom ‹ voice of bride
01

בָּרוּךְ אַתָּה יְהוָה אֱלֹהֵינוּ מֶלֶךְ הָעוֹלָם, שֶׁהַכֹּל בָּרָא לִכְבוֹדוֹ.

‹Who has created ««everything ««of the universe, ‹King ‹our God, ‹Hashem, ‹are You, ‹Blessed —— ««for His glory.*
(אָמֵן.) — All
01 / 07

לִכְבוֹדוֹ

for His glory

Commentary · Rashi

This blessing does not deal directly with marriage. It was instituted in honor of the guests who gathered to celebrate the marriage, for they emulate God Himself, Who served as a member of the wedding party in the Garden of Eden when Adam married Eve (Rashi, Kesubos 8a).

שֶׁהַכֹּל בָּרָא לִכְבוֹדוֹ
02

בָּרוּךְ אַתָּה יְהוָה אֱלֹהֵינוּ מֶלֶךְ הָעוֹלָם, יוֹצֵר הָאָדָם.

‹Who fashioned ««of the universe, ‹King ‹our God, ‹Hashem, ‹are You, ‹Blessed —— ««the Man.*
(אָמֵן.) — All
02 / 07

יוֹצֵר

Who fashioned

Commentary · Etz Yosef

Who fashioned the Man. This blessing refers to the creation of Adam, the first human being, and constitutes an introduction to the next blessing which thanks God for the creation of males and females (Rashi).

יוֹצֵר הָאָדָם
03

בָּרוּךְ אַתָּה יְהוָה אֱלֹהֵינוּ מֶלֶךְ הָעוֹלָם, אֲשֶׁר יָצַר אֶת הָאָדָם בְּצַלְמוֹ, בְּצֶלֶם דְּמוּת תַּבְנִיתוֹ, וְהִתְקִין לוֹ מִמֶּנּוּ בִּנְיַן עֲדֵי עַד. בָּרוּךְ אַתָּה יְהוָה, יוֹצֵר.

‹and ««of his likeness,* ‹in the semblance image ‹in His image,* ‹the Man ‹fashioned ‹Who —— ‹a building for eternity ‹from him
(אָמֵן.) — All
03 / 07

בְּצַלְמוֹ

in His image

Commentary · ArtScroll Genesis 1:26

In His image. The commentators generally agree that the Divine image refers to wisdom, intelligence, and free will. A building for eternity — Eve was created from Adam himself, from a part of Adam's body (Avodas Yisrael).

בִּנְיַן עֲדֵי עַד
‹ May she rejoice intensely ‹ the barren one ‹ and exult ‹ through the ingathering of her children ‹ amidst her «« Who gladdens ‹ Zion ‹ through her children ‹ Gladden intensely ‹ the companions ‹ who are beloved ‹ as You gladdened ‹ Your creation ‹ in the Garden of Eden ‹ May she rejoice intensely ‹ the barren one ‹ and exult ‹ through the ingathering of her children ‹ amidst her «« Who gladdens ‹ Zion ‹ through her children ‹ Gladden intensely ‹ the companions ‹ who are beloved ‹ as You gladdened ‹ Your creation ‹ in the Garden of Eden
4. — שׂוֹשׂ תָּשִׂישׂ

שׂוֹשׂ תָּשִׂישׂ* וְתָגֵל הָעֲקָרָה,* בְּקִבּוּץ בָּנֶיהָ לְתוֹכָהּ בְּשִׂמְחָה. בָּרוּךְ אַתָּה יְהוָה, מְשַׂמֵּחַ צִיּוֹן בְּבָנֶיהָ.

‹amidst ‹her children ‹ingathering ««— the barren one* ‹and exult ‹May she rejoice intensely* —— ««through her children.* ‹Zion ‹Who gladdens
(אָמֵן.) — All
Commentary · Etz Yosef

שׂוֹשׂ תָּשִׂישׂ — May she rejoice intensely. The repetition of the Hebrew verb indicates intensity and continuity. The same is true of the next blessing. Thus we pray that the joy and gladness of both Jerusalem and the new couple will be intense and never ending.

The barren one refers to Jerusalem, whose future joy is likened by the prophets to the joy of bride and groom (see Isaiah 62:5).

Commentary · Abudraham

שַׂמֵּחַ תְּשַׂמַּח — Gladden intensely. May God bring joy to the newly joined lives of the bride and groom. The companions who are beloved — the bride and groom who, through marriage, become loving companions to one another (Avodas Yisrael).

יְצִירְךָ — Your creation, Adam, whose lonely life was gladdened when Eve was formed and life was shared.

5. — שַׂמֵּחַ תְּשַׂמַּח

שַׂמֵּחַ תְּשַׂמַּח רֵעִים הָאֲהוּבִים,* כְּשַׂמֵּחֲךָ יְצִירְךָ* בְּגַן עֵדֶן מִקֶּדֶם.

‹Your creation, ‹as You gladdened ‹who are beloved,* ‹the companions ‹Gladden intensely* —— ‹in the Garden of Eden ‹from days of old.*
(אָמֵן.) — All
6. — Joy and Gladness · שָׂשׂוֹן וְשִׂמְחָה

בָּרוּךְ
אַתָּה יְהוָה אֱלֹהֵינוּ מֶלֶךְ הָעוֹלָם,
אֲשֶׁר בָּרָא שָׂשׂוֹן וְשִׂמְחָה,
חָתָן וְכַלָּה, גִּילָה רִנָּה,
דִּיצָה וְחֶדְוָה, אַהֲבָה וְאַחֲוָה,
שָׁלוֹם וְרֵעוּת.

‹delight, ‹pleasure, ‹glad song, ‹rejoicing, ‹and gladness, ‹groom and bride, ‹joy ‹love, ‹brotherhood, ‹peace, ‹companionship.*
07 BLESSINGS · ZIMUN · P.291

Joy… and companionship. Ten expressions of joy — alluding to the ten canopies God erected for Adam and Eve in the Garden of Eden (Bava Basra 75a).

אָמֵן
07
Leader holds the first cup

בָּרוּךְ אַתָּה יְהוָה אֱלֹהֵינוּ מֶלֶךְ הָעוֹלָם, בּוֹרֵא פְּרִי הַגָּפֶן.

‹Who creates ««of the universe, ‹King ‹our God, ‹Hashem, ‹are You, ‹Blessed —— ««of the vine. ‹the fruit
(אָמֵן.) — All
07 / 07

הַגָּפֶן

the vine

Note · Wine Mixing

The Leader drinks some of the wine from his cup; then wine from the two cups is mixed together and one cup is given to the groom and the other to the bride. It is laudable for those present to drink a bit of wine from the כּוֹס שֶׁל בְּרָכָה, cup of blessing, since it was used in the performance of a mitzvah.

‹ Who creates ‹ the fruit ‹ of the vine ‹ (Amen.) ‹ mixed together ‹ one cup to the groom ‹ one to the bride ‹ cup of blessing ‹ performance of a mitzvah ‹ Psalms 113:2 ‹ Cf. 23:3 ‹ Genesis 29:27 ‹ Judges 14:17 ‹ Isaiah 62:5 ‹ Jeremiah 33:10 ‹ Who creates ‹ the fruit ‹ of the vine ‹ (Amen.) ‹ mixed together ‹ one cup to the groom ‹ one to the bride ‹ cup of blessing ‹ performance of a mitzvah ‹ Psalms 113:2 ‹ Cf. 23:3 ‹ Genesis 29:27 ‹ Judges 14:17 ‹ Isaiah 62:5 ‹ Jeremiah 33:10
(1) Psalms 113:2 שָׂשׂוֹן וְשִׂמְחָה

Joy… and companionship. Ten expressions of joy allude to the ten canopies which God erected for Adam and Eve in the Garden of Eden (Bava Basra 75a).

(2) Cf. 23:3 יוֹצֵר הָאָדָם

Who fashioned the Man. This blessing refers to the creation of Adam, the first human being, and constitutes an introduction to the next blessing (Rashi).

(3) Genesis 1:26 בְּצַלְמוֹ

In His image. The commentators generally agree that the Divine image refers to wisdom, intelligence, and free will. The translation spells his with a lower case "h" following Abudraham.

(4) Isaiah 62:5 הָעֲקָרָה

The barren one — Jerusalem, whose future joy is likened by the prophets to the joy of bride and groom. How will God comfort Jerusalem? By gathering her children to her (Tanchuma; Abudraham).

followorbounce.com · 2026 שֶׁבַע בְּרָכוֹת Liturgical Typography Series
No. 02 / Sheva Berachos
